notes: Strategies for picking entries_per_run entries from goal-catalog.json each night. The SelfLearnCoordinator consults its config's slice.rotation_strategy to pick an implementation; quotas in config.slice.rotation_overrides are enforced on top.

weakest_first.description: Prioritize entries whose ledger rows show the lowest rubric mean, OR entries whose capability_match_rate is trending down in the last 14 days. This is the default — it directs compute where learning yields most.
signal_sources: RubricLedger rows windowed 14 days, grouped by entry_id, sorted by mean(rubricScore) ascending
Per-criterion failRate >= 0.5 in the same window
weakest_first.notes: Avoids the re-run-the-same-weak-entry-forever trap by allocating rotation_overrides.regressed_quota_per_run slots max. The balance comes from config.

never_tested.description: Entries that have zero ledger rows. Ensures new catalog additions get exercised within one rotation cycle regardless of their weakness profile.
never_tested.notes: This strategy is USUALLY applied as an override on top of whatever primary strategy is active — 3 slots of never-tested mixed into 9 slots of weakest_first.

recently_regressed.description: Entries whose ledger shows a downward score trend in the last 7 days (mean of last 3 rows < mean of prior 3). Fast detection of wisdom-rule regressions from overnight proposer merges.
signal_sources: RubricLedger windowed 7 days, entries with ≥6 rows, decreasing mean
recently_regressed.notes: Complements weakest_first — the weak ones stay weak; the regressed ones surface recent bad patches. If a freshly merged proposal caused a regression, this strategy surfaces it within 1-2 nights, letting LEARN auto-revert.

balanced_mix.description: Equal-ish distribution across category tags (productivity/education/games/creative/utility/finance/health/social). Used as a fallback when weakest_first has no signal yet (cold start).
balanced_mix.notes: Typical usage: week 1 cold-start cycles while the ledger fills, then switch to weakest_first once every entry has ≥3 rows.

round_robin.description: Simple cursor through the catalog in stable order. Boring but predictable. Useful for smoke-testing the coordinator itself.
round_robin.notes: Primarily for testing SelfLearnCoordinator SL.2. Not recommended for production.

composition.note: The coordinator ALWAYS runs a composed pick: primary strategy fills most slots, quota strategies fill named slots. Per-strategy quotas come from config.slice.rotation_overrides.

difficulty_mix_enforcement.notes: The strategy picks entry_ids; this secondary filter enforces the difficulty blend from config.slice.difficulty_mix. If the primary pick over-weighted L2 (say), the coordinator substitutes the weakest-ranked L1/L3 entries to hit the blend.
